provenance.task: PR 6 C2.1 — targeted cultural_heritage corrections applied to the Stage-2 re-tag output BEFORE the apply.
provenance.ruling_ref: B6 register adjudication (register-adjudication.md 'USER VERDICT NEEDED'): U-5 / U-8 / U-12 user verdicts 2026-06-15 (Session 11); Arroz from the PR-C1 heritage-filter map error-candidate flag.
provenance.verification: All 4 ids + their run cultural_heritage arrays cross-checked verbatim against scripts/stage2-retag/artifacts/full-run.fable.jsonl in Session 18 AND re-verified in this C2.1 build (each id present exactly once; run heritage matched the before-column exactly).
provenance.id_correction: The heritage-rebuild design doc originally named id 055b000b… for the Arroz fix; that id does NOT exist in the corpus (0 matches). The real spurious-South-Asian row is 1VShcRmc… (verified). Salsa Toasts get NO correction (user decision 2026-06-16).
provenance.policy: Heritage is stored specific-only (PR C1 recursive filter makes that safe). These corrections drop/clear specific spurious tags; they apply to fields['cultural_heritage'] BEFORE change-detection so a drop-to-empty row is still detected as changed and staged.
provenance.effect: prepare-apply.ts applies each correction to the run's cultural_heritage before building the staging row; the corrected value flows identically into the staging CSV/SQL and the draft apply migration (single source).
provenance.c2_u1_addendum: 2026-06-16 — added 7 'North American' DROP corrections (U-1 identity-anchor resolution). A read-only PROD probe found the C2 apply's U-1 hard-fail assertion would ABORT on real data: the re-tag's OWN output left the generic 'North American' label alongside an Indigenous-subtree label on 7 re-tagged lessons (12yRCn3m…, 12ZjWQ…, 16mgyKO8H…, 1ggAWmeMm…, 1zrWJ0…, lesson_03de6aa8…, lesson_341634b7…). Each id + its staged heritage was cross-verified against scripts/stage2-retag/artifacts/pr6-retag-staging.csv (column cultural_heritage) — every one carried BOTH 'North American' AND an Indigenous-subtree label. drop:['North American'] is robust to the exact remaining tags. Total corrections now = 11 (4 original + 7). The lone remaining violator (Soul Food Sunday, 1voTOBrqiz…) is a corpus-exclusion non-lesson handled by scoping the §7 assertion, not by a correction (it is not re-tagged).
